definition: Stock-bond correlation is endogenous to balance sheet policy with a maturity bifurcation diagnostic: 2Y-SPX correlation remains negative (~-0.15 to -0.30, tracking growth expectations) while 30Y-SPX has turned slightly positive (~+0.05 to +0.15, tracking supply/term premium). This bifurcation is diagnostic of the QT regime and has implications for maturity selection in hedging.
evidence: Identified in pair_0 debate as genuinely useful analytical contribution
open_questions: Will the 30Y-SPX positive correlation strengthen as QT continues?
What is the optimal maturity point for bond hedging of equity portfolios during QT?
